CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF MAY-04-2016 CONTINUED TO JUN-15-2016 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT FEI YU LI'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF'S NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT PROHIBITED UNDER CALIFORNIA LAW. (SEE CRUZ V. AYROMLOO (2007) 155 CAL.APP.4TH 1270, 1273.) PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUSTAIN ITS CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THAT PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT HAD A PREEXISTING RELATIONSHIP AND DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT CAUSED PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGED SEVERE EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT FEI YU LI'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MEET HIS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THE SECTIONS THAT ARE SOUGHT TO BE STRICKEN ARE "IRRELEVANT, FALSE OR IMPROPER," OR "NOT DRAWN IN CONFORMITY WITH THE LAWS OF THE STATE, COURT RULE OR ORDER OF THE COURT." (CCP 436.) P SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS FACTS THAT COULD LEAD A FINDER OF FACT TO DETERMINE THAT DEFENDANT'S CONDUCT WAS "DESPICABLE," AND NOT MERE CARELESSNESS OR IGNORANCE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-27-2016 Continued To Aug-10-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint
Off Calendar May-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FEI YU LI'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. Defendant failed to meet his burden to show that the sections that are sought to be stricken are "irrelevant, false or improper," or "not drawn in conformity with the laws of the state, court rule or order of the court." (CCP 436.) P sufficiently pleads facts that could lead a finder of fact to determine that Defendant's conduct was "despicable," and not mere carelessness or ignorance. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FEI YU LI'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OVERRULED. Plaintiff's negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is not prohibited under California law. (See Cruz v. Ayromloo (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1273.) Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to sustain its cause of action because Plaintiff alleges that Plaintiff and Defendant had a preexisting relationship and Defendant's conduct caused Plaintiff's alleged severe emotional distress. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
